steps.title: Substitute for t
steps.content:
Use $t=p^{2n-1}$ in the given equation to write both sides with base $p$:
$$
p^{3/8}=(p^{2n-1})^{5/6}
$$
$$
p^{3/8}=p^{\frac{5(2n-1)}{6}}
$$

steps.title: Set exponents equal
steps.content:
Since $p>1$, equal powers of $p$ have equal exponents:
$$
\frac{3}{8}=\frac{5(2n-1)}{6}
$$
$$
9=20(2n-1)
$$

steps.title: Solve for n
steps.content: Finish solving: $$ 9=40n-20 $$ $$ 29=40n $$ $$ n=\frac{29}{40} $$ The answer is 29/40.
